chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Dislike of Receiving Payment for Judging

text: 3227 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ قَاضٍ بَيْنَ قَرْيَتَيْنِ يَأْخُذُ مِنَ اَلسُّلْطَانِ عَلَى اَلْقَضَاءِ اَلرِّزْقَ فَقَالَ "ذَاكَ سُحْتٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3227 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan who said:
Abu Abdillah (as) was asked about a judge between two villages who takes a salary from the ruler for judging.
Imam (as) said: "That is unlawful (suht)."
